title: Research Funding Slows Again for Universities Targeted by White House
summary: The National Science Foundation lifted a hold on some grants for Harvard and other universities this week after inquiries from media outlets, including The New York Times.

title: Federal Judge Bars Trump From Immediately Setting Up $1.8 Billion Fund
summary: A judge in Virginia temporarily blocked the Trump administration from transferring money in or out of the fund until the court could hear arguments in June.

title: ICE Agent Wanted in Minneapolis Shooting Is Captured in Texas
summary: Christian Castro, who had been charged with assault in the shooting of a Venezuelan man during the immigration crackdown in Minnesota, was taken into custody Friday morning.

title: Who Is the Prosecutor Investigating E. Jean Carroll’s Lawsuits?
summary: Andrew S. Boutros, the Trump-appointed U.S. attorney for the Northern District of Illinois, is said to be overseeing the criminal investigation.

title: Inside the Room Where War Insurance Is Bought and Sold
summary: Many of the ships stranded in the Persian Gulf depend on coverage negotiated at Lloyd’s, the center of marine insurance for more than 300 years.

title: Israeli Troops Push Deeper Into Lebanon as Cease-Fire Talks Continue
summary: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Israeli ground forces had crossed Lebanon’s Litani River, as military officials from the two countries were set to meet for U.S.-brokered talks.

title: Louisiana Approves Map Eliminating a Majority-Black District
summary: A Supreme Court court ruling last month rejected the state’s previous congressional map as an illegal racial gerrymander and set off a redistricting race across the South.

title: How Bad Are President Trump’s Approval Ratings?
summary: Our chief political analyst, Nate Cohn, looks at President Trump’s sinking approval ratings. How do they compare with polling from other recent presidencies?

title: Gretchen Whitmer’s Approach to Trump Was Working With the President
summary: Ms. Whitmer, the Michigan governor and one of the country’s most prominent Democrats, has been conciliatory with Mr. Trump. As she mulls the future, her party is split on that approach.

title: The Politics of the Downwardly Mobile Professional Class
summary: The debate over whether Graham Platner is “working class” comes at a time when more and more people are at risk of falling into it.

title: Judge Tosses Citizenship Law Aimed at New Voters in New Hampshire
summary: The judge said the 2024 law “constitutes an unjustifiable burden on the right to vote,” and that new voters should be able to prove their citizenship with a sworn affidavit.

title: Russian Drone Hits Romanian Apartment Building, Officials Say
summary: Romania is a NATO country, and the security alliance condemned “Russia’s recklessness” for an episode that sharply escalated tensions with Moscow.

title: What to Know About the Drones That Have Been Crossing Into Romania
summary: The strike on Friday, near the Ukrainian border, was the first to injure civilians in Romania, officials say. But drones from the nearby war have been a growing threat.

title: Romania Says It Could Invoke NATO’s Article 4. What Would That Do?
summary: The clause in NATO’s founding treaty is an “instrument” Romania can use, the foreign minister said after a drone, alleged to be Russian, wounded two civilians.

title: For Wall Street, the Only Thing Worse Than SpaceX Flopping Is Missing Out
summary: Elon Musk and his bankers are working to create a self-fulfilling prophecy for the massive public offering: Make investors feel that the risks of passing it up outweigh those of buying into it.

title: Blue Origin Rocket Blows Up on Florida Launchpad During Test
summary: The rocket, built by the Jeff Bezos-owned space company, was to carry 48 satellites into space. Blue Origin reported on social media that “all personnel have been accounted for.”

title: Sky-High I.P.O. Pricing Isn’t Great for Real People
summary: When newly public companies have been valued as richly as SpaceX, OpenAI and Anthropic seem likely to be, the outlook for ordinary investors has been poor.

title: A Hidden Treasure of Rare Snake Specimens
summary: In the foothills of the Ecuadorean Amazon, a 101-year-old farmer and a young scientist turned an amateur collection into a scientific survey of one of the most diverse snake habitats on Earth.

title: Is Spencer Pratt for Real?
summary: The former reality star’s dark visions of Los Angeles are resonating in a heated mayoral race, even if they’re far from the truth.

title: Spencer Pratt Wants to Be Mayor. His Fans Want Him to Be Batman.
summary: What an A.I.-generated video of the former reality TV star teaches us about the future of political ads.

title: A.I. Doesn’t Have to Mean Layoffs
summary: A French multinational, Schneider Electric, decided to use artificial intelligence in manufacturing to make workers more productive, rather than to replace them. Here’s how that’s going.

title: The $900 Billion Giant: How Anthropic Got So Big, So Fast
summary: The artificial intelligence giant was just valued at $900 billion, surpassing OpenAI. Here are the numbers behind its rise — and headwinds it faces.

title: No, Not That Georgia. A.I. Compounds a Nation’s Identity Woes.
summary: Online queries are more likely to turn up references to the U.S. state — a problem for the republic in the Caucasus and its people. But a move to drop the name “Georgia” has lost steam.

title: Uber to Offer Shuttles for Fans Leaving World Cup Matches
summary: Soccer lovers frustrated by public transportation will have another option to return to Boston, Dallas, Miami and New York from hard-to-reach stadiums.

title: ‘Backrooms’ Is Part of a Boomlet in Movies From YouTube Creators
summary: “Backrooms,” a psychological horror flick opening this weekend, is part of a wave of breakout films from fledgling directors who honed their instincts on YouTube.

title: Kane Parsons: From YouTube Sensation to A24’s Youngest Director
summary: Now 20, the viral creator Kane Parsons is releasing “Backrooms,” a feature-length expansion of his web series.

title: Hungary Showed How to Defeat an Autocrat
summary: Eleven lessons America must learn

title: Does Trump Want to Lose the Midterms?
summary: The president doesn’t seem that concerned that his party could lose control of Congress. Ezra Klein and the Republican strategist Liam Donovan discuss Trump’s midterm strategy and Democratic paths to victory.

title: How NextEra Energy Wielded Political Power in Florida
summary: NextEra, which is seeking to buy Dominion Energy, has often butted heads with consumer groups, residents and journalists in its home state.

title: Can New Technology Help Save North Atlantic Right Whales?
summary: Innovative systems to keep ships from hitting North Atlantic right whales are coming into use. The Trump administration is weighing whether they can replace a bedrock protection.

title: Woman With No Right Hand Is Pulled Over for Using Phone in Her Right Hand
summary: Her case was, of course, dismissed.

title: One of Five Gold Miners Is Rescued From Laos Cave After More Than a Week
summary: An international effort to free the trapped men brought attention to the country’s informal mines, where panning for gold can pay more than minimum wage.
